<!--
Guidance for AI coding agents working on the `liushihowe.github.io` Jekyll blog repo.
Keep this file short (20-50 lines) and actionable. Update only when project conventions change.
-->

# Guidance for AI coding agents

This is a GitHub Pages site generated by Jekyll (theme: minima). The repository holds templates, includes and Markdown posts.

Key facts (quick):
- Site generator: Jekyll (see `_config.yml`).
- Deploy: pushing to `main` updates GitHub Pages at `https://liushihowe.github.io`.
- Posts: Markdown files live in `_posts/` and follow the `YYYY-MM-DD-title.md` convention.

What to know before editing:
- Templates are in `_layouts/` (notably `post.html` used to render posts).
- Partials are in `_includes/` (e.g. `head.html`, `navlinks.html`, `sharelinks.html`) and are referenced from layouts.
- Styles: `css/override.css` contains custom overrides; prefer small, targeted changes there.

Project-specific patterns and examples:
- Post front-matter: posts inherit defaults from `_config.yml` (author/tags/comments/toc). When creating posts, include `title`, `date`, and optionally `author`, `tags`, `comments`, `toc`.
  - Example post filename: `_posts/2025-09-17-github-commit-message.md`.
- Navigation: `navlinks.html` uses `page.previous` and `page.next` — maintain chronological order and correct front-matter dates for proper prev/next linking.
- Share buttons: `sharelinks.html` builds encoded `safeurl` and `safetitle` via Liquid filters; when changing sharing behavior, update this include.

Developer workflows (discoverable from repo):
- Local preview: use `jekyll serve` or GitHub Pages preview tools. Typical command:
  - `bundle exec jekyll serve --livereload` (ensure Ruby + bundler installed).
- Build artifacts: None committed — Jekyll generates `_site/` which is not tracked.

Conventions and constraints:
- Keep changes minimal to theme files; modify `_config.yml` only for site-level settings.
- Don't commit generated HTML from `_site/`.
- Use UTF-8 and keep Chinese content intact (site contains Chinese text and date formats like `%Y年%m月%d日`).

Integration points:
- Google Analytics ID is in `_config.yml` (`google_analytics`).
- Social usernames are wired via `_config.yml` (e.g. `github_username`).

When editing, reference these files:
- `_config.yml` — global defaults and plugins (jekyll-feed, jekyll-sitemap).
- `_layouts/post.html` — article template; shows how `sharelinks.html` and `navlinks.html` are used.
- `_includes/sharelinks.html` — code for share buttons and safe URL encoding.
- `css/override.css` — style overrides; prefer adjusting here for presentation fixes.
- `_posts/` — examples of post metadata and filename conventions.

Examples of actionable tasks an agent might do:
- Add a new post: create `_posts/YYYY-MM-DD-title.md` with front-matter and content.
- Fix prev/next links: ensure post dates are correct and regenerate site.
- Update share button logic: edit `_includes/sharelinks.html` and test with `jekyll serve`.

Edge cases to watch for:
- Changing Liquid tags or filters may affect multiple templates; run a local build to validate.
- Date formatting is localized — preserve the `{{ page.date | date: "%Y年%m月%d日" }}` pattern unless intentionally changing localization.

If unsure, leave large structural changes as a PR with description and a rendered preview.

---
Please review: which additional workflows or files should I call out (CI, custom plugins, or external services)?
